text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village, there lived a young girl named Ella. She had always dreamed of becoming an artist. Her summers were spent doodling on scraps of paper and sketching vibrant scenes of fantasy. One day, when ella was thirteen, she stumbled upon an old farmhouse amidst a thicket of nettles.

Inside was a treasure trove—a gallery of forgotten paintings. Ella picked up one canvas that seemed to shimmer softly. It depicted fields ablaze with sunlit flowers and gentle rainbows amidst clouds. Held tight within its folds was a character. They wore flowing robes and smiled warmly. Ella touched them—the painting felt warmer than air.

That night, Ella painted. She imagined her fairy friend. For months, she drew and rewrote tales about them. Her drawings grew bigger and more magical.

Then came the day everything changed. Ella's village held its annual festival. Her teacher encouraged everyone to showcase their creativity. Ella sketched her friend beside a river shimmering like jade
